q: Glucagon is released when blood glucose falls below the set point. What is its primary effect?
options: It stimulates beta cells to release more insulin as a countermeasure
It promotes glycogen synthesis in liver cells to store excess glucose
It stimulates glycogen breakdown (glycogenolysis) in the liver, releasing glucose into the blood
It inhibits cellular respiration to conserve the remaining glucose
explanation: Glucagon from alpha cells signals the liver to break down glycogen stores, releasing glucose into the blood to raise glucose concentration back to the set point.
